title: NBA Daily Fast Break
subtitle: Your NBA News Digest for May 22, 2026

items.headline: Knicks Dominate Cavaliers, Take Commanding 2-0 Eastern Conference Finals Lead
items.summary: The New York Knicks secured a pivotal 109-93 victory over the Cleveland Cavaliers in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference Finals on Thursday night, extending their series lead to 2-0. Josh Hart led the charge with a playoff career-high 26 points.

items.headline: Thunder Even Western Conference Finals with Spurs at 1-1 After Game 2 Victory
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder bounced back in Game 2 of the Western Conference Finals, defeating the San Antonio Spurs 122-113 to tie the series at 1-1. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander spearheaded the Thunder's effort with 30 points, 9 assists, and 4 rebounds.

items.headline: Key Injuries Impacting Conference Finals Matchups
items.summary: The San Antonio Spurs are facing injury concerns as De'Aaron Fox (right ankle sprain) and rookie Dylan Harper (right adductor soreness) are both listed as questionable for tonight's Game 3 against the Thunder. Oklahoma City's Jalen Williams, who experienced left hamstring tightness in Game 2, is expected to return today.

items.headline: Knicks vs. Cavaliers - ECF Game 2
items.summary: The New York Knicks extended their Eastern Conference Finals lead to 2-0 with a dominant 109-93 victory over the Cleveland Cavaliers at Madison Square Garden. Josh Hart led all scorers with a playoff career-high 26 points.

items.headline: Thunder vs. Spurs - WCF Game 3
items.summary: The Western Conference Finals shift to San Antonio tonight for Game 3, with the series tied 1-1. The Thunder will look to build on their Game 2 win, while the Spurs aim to regain home-court advantage.

items.headline: Josh Hart's Playoff Career-High
items.statContext: NYK G Josh Hart's stellar performance helped the Knicks to a 2-0 ECF lead.

items.headline: Brunson's Double-Double Dimes
items.statContext: NYK G Jalen Brunson showcased his playmaking and scoring in the Knicks' Game 2 victory.

items.headline: SGA Leads Thunder Comeback
items.statContext: OKC G Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's MVP-caliber outing tied the Western Conference Finals.

items.headline: Wembanyama's Dominant Stat Line
items.statContext: SAS C Victor Wembanyama continued to fill the stat sheet in Game 2 of the WCF.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Strong 2025-26 Season
items.statContext: The Israeli forward for the Portland Trail Blazers had a productive season, showcasing his all-around game.

items.headline: Dylan Harper's Historic Rookie Postseason Run
items.statContext: Spurs rookie Dylan Harper delivered a rare playoff stat line, joining elite company like Magic Johnson. He was also named to the NBA All-Rookie First Team.

items.headline: 2025-26 NBA All-Rookie First Team Announced
items.summary: VJ Edgecombe (PHI), Cooper Flagg (DAL), Kon Knueppel (CHA), Dylan Harper (SAS), and Cedric Coward (MEM) were named to the NBA All-Rookie First Team for the 2025-26 season. Edgecombe and Flagg were unanimous selections.

items.headline: Maxime Raynaud Highlights NBA All-Rookie Second Team
items.summary: Sacramento Kings center Maxime Raynaud, along with Jeremiah Fears (NOP), Derik Queen (NOP), Ace Bailey (UTA), and Collin Murray-Boyles (TOR), earned a spot on the 2025-26 NBA All-Rookie Second Team. Raynaud averaged 12.5 points and 7.5 rebounds in his debut season.

items.headline: Former NBA Player Darius Songaila Injured in Violent Attack
items.summary: Darius Songaila, former NBA player and current BC Siauliai head coach, suffered facial injuries after allegedly being attacked with a dog leash chain in Lithuania. The incident occurred while he was protecting his small dog from another aggressive dog.
